title: Affirm API credentials should not be disclosed
description: Affirm is a buy-now-pay-later payment platform used by e-commerce merchants. Affirm credentials include public/private key pairs. The private key is used to sign checkout requests and authenticate API calls. Exposing private keys allows fraudulent loan origination and order manipulation.
recommendation: Remove Affirm credentials from source code. Store private keys in environment variables. Rotate at merchant.affirm.com.
impact: Unauthorized loan origination, order management, and access to customer financing data. PCI-DSS compliance violation.

checks.message: Affirm API private/secret key detected in variable assignment. Affirm private keys sign checkout session requests and authenticate merchant API calls for buy-now-pay-later transactions. Exposing this key enables fraudulent checkout session creation, unauthorized order captures and refunds, and access to customer financing data. This is a PCI-DSS compliance violation. Rotate immediately at merchant.affirm.com.
